curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 5 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Identify fundamental and derived SI units and systems of measurement.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships.
Analyze uniform and non-uniform motion using differentiation and integration concepts.
Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs to determine displacement and acceleration.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components and perform vector operations (addition, subtraction, dot/cross products).
Derive equations for projectile motion and analyze cases of uniform circular motion.
Explain the intuitive concept of force, inertia, and impulse in relation to motion changes.
Apply Newton's three laws of motion to solve equilibrium and dynamic problems involving concurrent forces.
Analyze static and kinetic friction, rolling friction, and lubrication effects.
Calculate centripetal force required for uniform circular motion in various contexts (level roads, banked curves).
Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using graphical and integral methods.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way.

visually_impaired_students.description: 30 minutes duration, 15 questions (answer any 10), skill-based very short answer type.

visually_impaired_students.requirement: Record at least five of the listed experiments in a practical file checked and signed by internal examiner.

visually_impaired_students.description: Questions based on basic theory/principle/concept, apparatus/materials/chemicals required, procedure, precautions, sources of error etc.

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by students. Suggested projects include studying factors on internal resistance/EMF, LDR variations, refractive indices, transformer relations, angle of deviation in hollow prism, induced charge estimation, self-inductance dependence, and earth's magnetic field.

marks_distribution: Class XI/XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 hours
Class XI/XII Practical: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hours (Visually Impaired: 2 hours)

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way.

class_xi_xii_general.component: Viva on experiments, activities and project

visually_impaired_students.description: 30 minutes duration, 15 questions (answer any 10), skill-based very short answer type.

visually_impaired_students.requirement: Record at least five of the listed experiments in a practical file checked and signed by internal examiner.

visually_impaired_students.description: Questions based on basic theory/principle/concept, apparatus/materials/chemicals required, procedure, precautions, sources of error etc.

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by students. Suggested projects include studying factors on internal resistance/EMF, LDR variations, refractive indices, transformer relations, angle of deviation in hollow prism, induced charge estimation, self-inductance dependence, and earth's magnetic field.

competency_groups.title: Measurement and Dimensional Analysis
competency_groups.description: Understanding units, significant figures, uncertainty, dimensional analysis, and their applications.
competencies: Identify fundamental and derived SI units and systems of measurement.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics in One and Two Dimensions
competency_groups.description: Describing motion using calculus, vectors, graphs, projectile motion, and circular motion.
competencies: Analyze uniform and non-uniform motion using differentiation and integration concepts.
Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs to determine displacement and acceleration.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components and perform vector operations (addition, subtraction, dot/cross products).
Derive equations for projectile motion and analyze cases of uniform circular motion.

competency_groups.title: Dynamics and Laws of Motion
competency_groups.description: Understanding forces, inertia, Newton's laws, momentum conservation, friction, and circular dynamics.
competencies: Explain the intuitive concept of force, inertia, and impulse in relation to motion changes.
Apply Newton's three laws of motion to solve equilibrium and dynamic problems involving concurrent forces.
Analyze static and kinetic friction, rolling friction, and lubrication effects.
Calculate centripetal force required for uniform circular motion in various contexts (level roads, banked curves).

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy, and Power Principles
competency_groups.description: Relating work done by forces to kinetic energy, potential energy, conservation laws, and collisions.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using graphical and integral methods.
Apply the Work-Energy Theorem to relate net work to changes in kinetic energy.
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces and analyze potential energy concepts (including springs).
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions conserving momentum and energy.

competency_groups.title: Rotational Dynamics and Rigid Body Motion
competency_groups.description: Analyzing center of mass, torque, angular momentum, moment of inertia, and rotational equations.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for particle systems and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rods).
Apply conservation of linear and angular momentum to solve collision and interaction problems.
Calculate torque and analyze equilibrium conditions for rigid bodies under concurrent forces.
Compare linear and rotational motion variables, calculate moment of inertia for simple geometries.
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation and Orbital Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Understanding universal gravitation, gravitational fields/potentials, and satellite motion.
competencies: Apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion to describe orbital trajectories.
Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.
Calculate gravitational potential energy, escape speed, and orbital velocity for satellites.

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids and Fluids
competency_groups.description: Exploring elasticity, stress-strain relationships, fluid pressure, viscosity, surface tension, and Bernoulli's principle.
competencies: Apply Hooke's Law to analyze elastic behavior, Young's modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus, and Poisson's ratio.
Calculate hydrostatic pressure variations and apply Pascal's law in hydraulic systems.
Analyze fluid dynamics including viscosity (Stokes' law), terminal velocity, streamline/turbulent flow regimes.
Apply Bernoulli's theorem to explain dynamic lift and Torricelli's law.

competency_groups.title: Thermal Physics and Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Understanding heat transfer, thermal expansion, specific heats, laws of thermodynamics, and gas behavior.
competencies: Analyze modes of heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation) and Blackbody radiation concepts.
Apply the Zeroth Law to define temperature equilibrium.
Use First and Second Laws of Thermodynamics to analyze isothermal, adiabatic, reversible, irreversible, and cyclic processes.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases
competency_groups.description: Connecting macroscopic gas properties to microscopic molecular behavior and statistical mechanics.
competencies: Derive the equation of state for a perfect gas from kinetic theory assumptions.
Interpret temperature as an average measure of molecular kinetic energy (rms speed).
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities of gases.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations and Wave Phenomena
competency_groups.description: Analyzing simple harmonic motion, energy in oscillators, pendulums, wave propagation, superposition, and interference.
competencies: Describe periodic functions and derive equations for Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M).
Analyze the dynamics of loaded springs and simple pendulums including time period derivations.
Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and apply the principle of superposition.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics: Fields, Potentials, and Capacitance
competency_groups.description: Understanding charge interactions, electric fields via Coulomb's law and Gauss's theorem, potentials, and capacitor systems.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's Law and the superposition principle to calculate forces between point charges.
Use Gauss's Theorem to determine electric fields for symmetric charge distributions (wire, plane, shell).
Calculate electric potential and energy for various configurations including dipoles.

competency_groups.title: Capacitors and Dielectrics
competency_groups.description: Analyzing potential energy, dielectric effects on capacitance, and series/parallel combinations.
competencies: Analyze the effect of dielectrics on electric fields and capacitor performance (capacitance increase).
Calculate equivalent capacitance for complex networks in series and parallel.

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity Fundamentals
competency_groups.description: Understanding drift velocity, Ohm's law, resistivity, internal resistance, Kirchhoff's laws, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Relate electric current to microscopic parameters (drift velocity, mobility).
Analyze V-I characteristics for linear and non-linear components.

competency_groups.title: Magnetic Effects of Moving Charges
competency_groups.description: Exploring magnetic fields, Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, forces on charges/conductors, and galvanometers.
competencies: Apply the Biot-Savart Law to calculate magnetic fields from current loops.
Use Ampere's Circuital Law for infinite wires and solenoids.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism in Matter
competency_groups.description: Classifying magnetic materials, understanding dipoles, torque on magnets, and temperature effects.
competencies: Distinguish between para-, dia-, and ferro-magnetic substances based on their properties.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction and AC Circuits
competency_groups.description: Understanding induction laws, self/mutual inductance, LCR circuits, resonance, power factor, and transformers.
competencies: Apply Faraday's Law and Lenz's Law to determine induced EMF and current direction.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current Analysis
competency_groups.description: Analyzing RMS values, reactance/impedance in LCR circuits, resonance conditions, and power calculations.
competencies: Analyze phasor diagrams for series LCR circuits to determine phase differences.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves and Spectrum
competency_groups.description: Understanding displacement current, wave characteristics, transverse nature, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Explain the generation of EM waves via changing electric/magnetic fields.

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
competency_groups.description: Analyzing reflection, refraction, total internal reflection, lenses, mirrors, prisms, and optical instruments.
competencies: Apply mirror formulae and lens maker's formula to solve image formation problems.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics: Interference and Diffraction
competency_groups.description: Understanding Huygen's principle, interference patterns (Young's experiment), diffraction phenomena.
competencies: Apply the concept of superposition to explain constructive and destructive interference.

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.description: Understanding photoelectric effect, particle nature of light, de-Broglie waves.
competencies: Explain the experimental evidence for the particle nature of light (photoelectric effect).
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Nuclear Physics
competency_groups.description: Analyzing Rutherford/Bohr models, hydrogen spectra, nuclear forces, mass-energy equivalence, fission/fusion.
competencies: Describe the Bohr model of the atom including quantization of orbits.

competency_groups.title: Semiconductor Electronics
competency_groups.description: Understanding energy bands, p-n junctions, diode characteristics, rectification.
competencies: Analyze the I-V characteristics of a semiconductor diode in forward and reverse bias.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
